channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Left-leaning independent Connolly wins Irish presidential election
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Left-wing independent Catherine Connolly, who secured the backing of Ireland's left-leaning parties including Sinn Féin, has won the country's presidential election in a landslide victory against her center-right rival.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Storm Melissa reaches hurricane strength, threatening catastrophic flooding in northern Caribbean
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. forecasters issued a hurricane warning for Jamaica Saturday as Storm Melissa reached hurricane strength, threatening catastrophic flooding in the northern Caribbean. A hurricane warning means winds of at least 74 mph (119 kph) are expected in the area within 36 hours.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge rules immigration detention of Chicago man with daughter battling cancer is illegal
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The detention by immigration authorities of a Chicago man whose 16-year-old daughter is undergoing treatment for advanced cancer is illegal, and he must be given a bond hearing by Oct. 31, a federal judge has ruled. Attorneys for Ruben Torres Maldonado, 40, who was detained Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro says the US is fabricating a war as aircraft carrier approaches
item.description: CARACAS (AP) — Venezuela’s President Nicolás Maduro said the United States government is forging a war against him as the world's biggest warship approaches the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI indicts dozens of people in Philadelphia on drug charges
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — More than two dozen people have been indicted on drug-related charges as part of a yearslong investigation into a gang in Philadelphia, the Federal Bureau of Investigation announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: DHS marks 'one of the most violent days' of Operation Midway Blitz with several arrests
item.description: Homeland Security details "one of the most violent days" of Operation Midway Blitz amid President Donald trump's crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Company recalls 2 million pounds of barbecue pork jerky that may contain metal wire
item.description: (The Associated Press) -- A South Dakota company is recalling more than 2.2 million pounds (998,000 kilograms) of Korean barbecue pork jerky sold at Costco and Sam's Club stores because the product may be contaminated with pieces of metal, federal health officials said Friday. LSI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Fox 5

item.title: By the Numbers: Why trick-or-treaters may bag more gummy candy than chocolate this Halloween
item.description: Trick-or-treaters may find more fruity candy than chocolate among their Halloween handouts this year.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, WGN 9

item.title: Congresswoman scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 99-year-old WWII veteran shares culinary talents
item.description: Every week, Vincent Martinez can be found cooking for others, a passion that traces back to his time in the Navy during World War II.
item.dc:creator: Redrick Terry, NewsNation

item.title: Girl shot in Minneapolis church returns home from the hospital
item.description: A 12-year-old girl who was shot in the head during a deadly attack at a Minneapolis Catholic church in August was released from the hospital Thursday and greeted with cheers as the police chief paraded her around town in a stretch limousine, according to the Minneapolis Star Tribune.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iron Maiden returns to Mexico City as part of 2026 'Run for Your Lives' tour
item.description: The iconic Iron Maiden rock band is bringing its "Run For Your Lives" tour to Mexico City next year, the group first visited Mexico 34 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana man arrested in child porn investigation
item.description: A 21-year-old Shreveport man was arrested and faces ten charges related to the possession of juvenile pornography after a search warrant was executed at his residence.
item.dc:creator: Erykah Agers, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama executes man with nitrogen gas for 1993 murder over $200 drug debt
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted of helping to burn a man alive in 1993 over a $200 drug debt was executed by nitrogen gas on Thursday. Anthony Boyd, 54, was pronounced dead at 6:33 p.m. at William C. Holman Correctional Facility, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alaska Airlines flights grounded due to IT outage
item.description: (KRON) -- Alaska Airlines has temporarily grounded all its flights due to an IT outage late Thursday afternoon, the airline announced on social media. As of 8 p.m., the airline is "actively restoring (its) operations following an IT outage," Alaska Airlines posted on X.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Nationwide auto theft ring leads to Bay Area car business: CA AG
item.description: (KRON) -- Forty felony charges were filed Thursday against eight people tied to a nationwide auto theft ring and "complex" altered vehicle identification number scheme. The suspects then sold the 19 stolen cars out of their Bay Area business, announced California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: ICE looking to hire over 40 health care workers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is hiring for more than 40 medical positions in its immigration detention centers across the nation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: A timeline of US attacks off South America and what Congress has had to say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In less than two months, President Donald Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth say the U.S. military has killed at least 37 people in nine strikes against drug-smuggling vessels in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines may help some cancer patients fight tumors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The most widely used COVID-19 vaccines may offer a surprise benefit for some cancer patients – revving up their immune systems to help fight tumors.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC woman says she lives in ‘constant fear’ after months-long ICE detention
item.description: “I lost three months of my life. I don’t want to [lose] my life again. I live in constant fear of being re-arrested,” Kostak said. “I don’t want to go back there.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WGN 9

item.title: Spirit Christmas is expanding: 30 locations announced across 13 states, including Illinois
item.description: (NEXSTAR/WHTM) – It may seem like Spirit Halloween has only just taken over your nearest shuttered Rite Aid and Party City locations, but some may soon become Spirit Christmas locations within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Spirit Christmas is expanding: 30 locations announced across 13 states
item.description: (NEXSTAR/WHTM) – It may seem like Spirit Halloween has only just taken over your nearest shuttered Rite Aid and Party City locations, but some may soon become Spirit Christmas locations within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Chess grandmaster Daniel Naroditsky is remembered as a leader in the game's online surge
item.description: Daniel Naroditsky, a 29-year-old standard-bearer in the world of competitive speed chess that flourished in the COVID-19 pandemic, died over the weekend and leaves behind a legacy as one of the greats of the game who helped usher in its digital era.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: An elephant family smashed pumpkins at the Oregon Zoo. But this baby just wanted to play ball
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A baby elephant at the Oregon Zoo had more tricks than treats to show when handlers gave it a small pumpkin to play with during an annual fall event where giant elephants smash half-ton pumpkins.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pick to lead federal watchdog agency withdraws after offensive text messages were revealed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump ’s pick to lead a federal watchdog agency withdrew from consideration Tuesday evening, after his offensive text messages were made public and GOP senators revolted.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas couple accused of moving, leaving child behind
item.description: A 12-year-old Texas boy came home from school to find it empty and his family gone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: Californians have until midnight to register to vote in Nov. 4 special election
item.description: Californians have until midnight tonight to register to vote in the statewide special election on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. pushes Utah GOP redistricting repeal efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump's son, Donald Trump Jr. is pushing his followers to sign up for signature gathering efforts for the Utah's GOP's dual-track effort to repeal Utah's anti-gerrymandering process and the court-stipulated map that Utah lawmakers approved earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, ABC 4
